calendarNotes:
Fresno Unified School District begins the 2025-2026 school year on August 18, 2025 — several weeks earlier than most coastal California districts, which typically start in late August or after Labor Day. The early calendar is not coincidental: Fresno's summer temperatures regularly exceed 105°F, and starting in mid-August allows the school year to conclude before the most oppressive heat of June.

Spring Break runs March 30–April 6, 2026, and the school year ends June 11, 2026. Families relocating from coastal California or other states that start after Labor Day should plan August preparations — school enrollment, childcare, and back-to-school shopping — well ahead of the mid-August start.

California public school calendars include several holidays that may be unfamiliar to families arriving from other states. Lincoln's Birthday (observed on February 9, 2026) is a paid school holiday in Fresno Unified — not recognized in most other states. Cesar Chavez Day (March 31) is a California state school holiday; in years when it falls outside Spring Break, it becomes an additional no-school day.

Fresno Unified also schedules District Buyback Days throughout the year — days when teachers attend professional development while students stay home. These are listed on the official calendar and typically fall on Fridays or adjacent to existing breaks. Unlike many districts in other states, Fresno Unified does not have a Fall Break. The major multi-day breaks are Thanksgiving (approximately one week in late November), Winter Break (approximately three weeks beginning December 22), and Spring Break.

Winter Tule fog is the weather event Fresno families plan around — not snow. Dense ground fog rolls through the San Joaquin Valley from November through February, sometimes reducing visibility to near zero and causing school bus delays, staggered arrival times, or school closures. Tule fog can develop overnight and lift unpredictably, so Fresno Unified communicates delays through its parent notification system — families should confirm their contact preferences are current before November. In fall and late summer, wildfire smoke from Sierra Nevada fires regularly moves into the valley. On high-AQI days, Fresno Unified may move outdoor PE classes indoors and shorten recess. Families with children who have asthma or respiratory conditions should ensure the school nurse has updated medical documentation at the start of each year.

Fresno sits approximately 65 miles from Yosemite's South Entrance — roughly a 90-minute drive under normal conditions. Spring Break in late March and early April falls outside Yosemite's peak summer crowds, making it a popular time for Central Valley families to visit. Yosemite Valley day-use reservations open on a rolling 28-day window, meaning March 30 reservations become available around March 2. Set a calendar reminder for early March to book as soon as the window opens — weekend reservations can fill quickly, weekday slots tend to last longer. This proximity to Yosemite is one of the genuine geographic advantages of living in Fresno.

Fresno Unified operates one of California's most linguistically and culturally diverse school systems, reflecting the demographic reality of the San Joaquin Valley. The district runs Dual Language Immersion programs in Spanish and Hmong — two of the most widely spoken home languages in Fresno — alongside standard English instruction at multiple campuses. At the high school level, Fresno Unified offers Career and Technical Education (CTE) pathways across health science, business, digital media, and agriculture. The district's Agricultural Education program is among the most established in California, a natural extension of the valley's identity as the most productive farming region in the United States — students in Fresno's agricultural programs compete in FFA events tied directly to real crop and livestock cycles in the surrounding region. Families interested in specialized programs should contact Fresno Unified's school choice office in the fall; enrollment windows for specialty schools and programs typically open before the end of the calendar year for the following school year.
